title: Planning a Holiday
summary: A woman and a man discuss plans for their upcoming holiday, including the destination, accommodation, and activities they want to do.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: OK, so we need to decide where we're going for our holiday this year.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: I've been thinking about that. What about somewhere by the sea? I'd love to go to a beach destination.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: That sounds nice, but it's always so crowded in summer. What about the mountains instead?

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Hmm, I don't mind the mountains, but I was really hoping for some warm weather and water sports.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: How about we go in September? The beaches are much quieter and the weather is still warm.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: That's a great idea. September would work well. Have you looked at any places?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Yes, I found a nice coastal town about three hours' drive from here. It has a beautiful bay and lots of restaurants.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: That sounds perfect. What about accommodation? Should we book a hotel or rent a house?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: I think renting a house is better. It's cheaper and we can cook our own meals.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Good point. We could save money on food and spend it on activities instead.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Exactly. I'd like to do some snorkelling and maybe hire kayaks for a day.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: I'm in! I also want to explore the local markets and try some seafood.

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Perfect. I'll start looking at rental properties online tonight. How long should we go for?

body.speaker: Man
body.text: I can take ten days off work. Is that enough?

body.speaker: Woman
body.text: Ten days is plenty. I'll book the house and you can look at things to do.

body.speaker: Man
body.text: Deal. I'm really excited — this is going to be a brilliant holiday.

annotations.exampleEn: Thailand is a popular beach destination for tourists.
The beach gets very crowded in summer.

annotations.meaningVi: các môn thể thao dưới nước
annotations.exampleEn: The resort offers many water sports, including windsurfing and diving.
They stayed in a quiet coastal town for the weekend.
We need to book accommodation before the prices go up.
It is often cheaper to rent a house than to stay in a hotel.
We went snorkelling and saw colourful fish.
You can hire kayaks at the beach for twenty dollars an hour.

annotations.meaningVi: Tôi đồng ý / Tôi tham gia
annotations.exampleEn: 'We're going to the cinema tonight.' 'I'm in!'
Visiting local markets is a great way to discover local food.

annotations.meaningVi: các bất động sản cho thuê
annotations.exampleEn: She searched for rental properties near the beach.

grammarNotes.title: 'What about + noun/verb-ing' and 'How about + noun/verb-ing' for suggestions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'What about + danh từ / động danh từ (-ing)' hoặc 'How about + danh từ / động danh từ (-ing)' để đưa ra gợi ý. 'What about the mountains instead?' — Thế còn núi thì sao? 'How about we go in September?' — Thế chúng ta đi vào tháng Chín thì sao? Đây là cách diễn đạt rất thông dụng và thân thiện trong hội thoại. Cả hai cấu trúc đều có thể dùng thay thế nhau.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'What about + noun/gerund' or 'How about + noun/gerund' to make suggestions. Examples: 'What about the mountains instead?' / 'How about we go in September?' Both are informal and friendly. 'What about' is slightly more common in British English; 'How about' is widely used in American English. You can also say 'Why don't we + base verb?' as another option.

grammarNotes.title: Comparative adjective + noun: 'the beaches are much quieter'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'much' trước tính từ so sánh hơn để nhấn mạnh sự khác biệt. 'The beaches are much quieter in September' — Các bãi biển yên tĩnh hơn rất nhiều vào tháng Chín. 'It's cheaper' — Rẻ hơn. Có thể thêm 'much', 'a lot', 'far', 'a little', 'slightly' trước so sánh hơn: 'much cheaper', 'a lot nicer', 'slightly better'. Đây là cách làm câu so sánh tự nhiên và sinh động hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use intensifiers before comparative adjectives to strengthen the comparison. 'Much' shows a big difference: 'The beaches are much quieter in September.' Other intensifiers: 'a lot quieter', 'far cheaper', 'a little warmer', 'slightly better.' Avoid using 'very' before a comparative — it is incorrect. Say 'much better' not 'very better.'

translationVi:
Phụ nữ: OK, vậy chúng ta cần quyết định đi đâu cho kỳ nghỉ năm nay.
Đàn ông: Tôi đã nghĩ về điều đó. Thế còn đi đâu đó gần biển thì sao? Tôi rất muốn đến một điểm đến bãi biển.
Phụ nữ: Nghe hay đó, nhưng mùa hè luôn đông đúc lắm. Thế còn đến núi thì sao?
Đàn ông: Hmm, núi thì cũng được, nhưng tôi thực sự mong có thời tiết ấm áp và các môn thể thao dưới nước.
Phụ nữ: Thế chúng ta đi vào tháng Chín thì sao? Các bãi biển yên tĩnh hơn nhiều và thời tiết vẫn còn ấm.
Đàn ông: Ý tưởng hay đấy. Tháng Chín sẽ phù hợp. Bạn đã xem qua những nơi nào chưa?
Phụ nữ: Rồi, tôi tìm thấy một thị trấn ven biển đẹp cách đây khoảng ba tiếng lái xe. Nơi đó có một vịnh đẹp và nhiều nhà hàng.
Đàn ông: Nghe hoàn hảo quá. Còn chỗ ở thì sao? Chúng ta nên đặt khách sạn hay thuê nhà?
Phụ nữ: Tôi nghĩ thuê nhà tốt hơn. Rẻ hơn và chúng ta có thể tự nấu ăn.
Đàn ông: Đúng vậy. Chúng ta có thể tiết kiệm tiền ăn và chi cho các hoạt động.
Phụ nữ: Đúng vậy. Tôi muốn đi lặn ống thở và có thể thuê kayak một ngày.
Đàn ông: Tôi đồng ý! Tôi cũng muốn khám phá các chợ địa phương và thử hải sản.
Phụ nữ: Hoàn hảo. Tối nay tôi sẽ bắt đầu tìm kiếm các bất động sản cho thuê trực tuyến. Chúng ta nên đi bao lâu?
Đàn ông: Tôi có thể nghỉ phép mười ngày. Thế có đủ không?
Phụ nữ: Mười ngày là đủ rồi. Tôi sẽ đặt nhà và bạn có thể xem các hoạt động để làm.
Đàn ông: Đồng ý. Tôi thực sự rất hứng khởi — đây sẽ là một kỳ nghỉ tuyệt vời.

questions.prompt: What type of holiday does the man want?
questions.explanation: 'I'd love to go to a beach destination.'
questions.hint: He mentions wanting warm weather and water.

questions.prompt: Why does the woman not want to go in summer?
options: She cannot take time off work then
questions.hint: She talks about too many people.

questions.prompt: When does the woman suggest going?
questions.explanation: 'How about we go in September? The beaches are much quieter and the weather is still warm.'

questions.prompt: How far is the coastal town from where they live?
questions.explanation: 'A coastal town about three hours' drive from here.'
questions.hint: A specific driving time is given.

questions.prompt: What type of accommodation does the woman prefer?
questions.explanation: 'I think renting a house is better. It's cheaper and we can cook our own meals.'
questions.hint: She gives two reasons for her choice.

questions.prompt: Why does the man agree that renting is better?
options: They can save money on food and spend it on activities
It is closer to the beach
questions.explanation: 'We could save money on food and spend it on activities instead.'

questions.prompt: What water activity does the woman want to do?
questions.hint: You use a tube and a mask for this activity.

questions.prompt: What does the man want to do at the local markets?
questions.explanation: 'I also want to explore the local markets and try some seafood.'

questions.prompt: Who will look for a rental property?
questions.explanation: 'I'll start looking at rental properties online tonight.'
questions.hint: She volunteers to do this task.

questions.prompt: How many days off work can the man take?
questions.explanation: 'I can take ten days off work.'

questions.prompt: What will the man do while the woman books the house?
questions.explanation: 'I'll book the house and you can look at things to do.'

questions.prompt: How does the man feel about the holiday plans at the end?
questions.explanation: 'I'm really excited — this is going to be a brilliant holiday.'

Man: What about somewhere by the sea? I'd love to go to a beach destination.
Woman: That sounds nice, but it's always so {{b2}} in summer. What about the mountains instead?
Man: I was really hoping for some warm weather and water {{b3}}.

Man: That's a great idea. Have you looked at any places?
Woman: Yes, I found a nice {{b5}} town about three hours' drive from here. It has a beautiful bay and lots of restaurants.
Man: What about accommodation? Should we book a hotel or {{b6}} a house?
Woman: I think renting a house is better. It's cheaper and we can cook our own meals.
Man: We could save money on food and spend it on {{b7}} instead.
Woman: Exactly. I'd like to do some snorkelling and maybe hire {{b8}} for a day.
Man: I'm in! I also want to explore the local markets and try some {{b9}}.

Man: I can take ten days off work. Is that enough?
Woman: Ten days is plenty. I'll book the house and you can look at things to {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: 'holiday' — they are planning their annual holiday.
'crowded' — the woman says it is always 'so crowded in summer.'
'sports' — 'water sports' are activities done in or on water.
'quieter' — the passage says 'the beaches are much quieter' in September.
'coastal' — a coastal town is a town by the sea.
'rent' — 'rent a house' means to pay to use it temporarily.
'activities' — they plan to spend saved money on things to do.
'kayaks' — the passage says 'maybe hire kayaks for a day.'
'seafood' — the man says he wants to 'try some seafood.'
'do' — 'things to do' is a common phrase for activities at a destination.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The couple in this conversation plan carefully before their holiday — choosing the right time of year, type of accommodation, and activities. How important is it to plan a holiday in advance? What are the advantages and disadvantages of planning everything versus making decisions as you travel?
